revision.text:
[[ja:大水神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17223392]] Wikidata name: Ōmizu Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōmizu Shrine, Kawaai Shrine, Kumabuchi Shrine |image= [[File:Ōmizu-jinja 01.jpg|270px]] |location= [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], Uji Imazai-kechō, Hayasakiguchi 163-1, [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 27 |latitude_sec = 33.8 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 43 |longitude_sec = 17.5 |map= Japan Mie |enshrined_deity= Ōmizu Shrine: Ōyamazumi-no-Mioya-no-Mikoto
Kawaai Shrine: Hosokawa Mizu-no-Kami
Kumabuchi Shrine: Takiootogi-no-Kami |status= Ōmizu Shrine: {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine
Kawaai Shrine & Kumabuchi Shrine: Kōtai Jingū subordinate shrines |established= Traditionally attributed to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] |main_hall_style= [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |alternate_names= |sacred_sites= |annual_festival= |rituals= [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] }} '''Ōmizu Shrine''' (大水神社) is an auxiliary shrine of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|zh|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. This article also covers the subordinate shrines '''Kawaai Shrine''' (川相神社) and '''Kumabuchi Shrine''' (熊淵神社), which are co-located with Ōmizu Shrine. All three shrines are said to have been established by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]. Ōmizu Shrine enshrines a {{ill|Mountain God|ja|山の神|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mountain God|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Yama-no-Kami|WD=Q839254}}, while Kawaai Shrine and Kumabuchi Shrine enshrine [[:en:Suijin|water god]].Gakken Publishing (2013): pp. 54–55 == Overview == These three shrines are located at Uji Imazai-kechō, Hayasakiguchi 163-1, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], about 200 meters from the {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise City)|lt=Uji Bridge (Ise City)|lt=Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji Bridge (Ise)|qq=}} at the entrance to the Inner Shrine's sacred precinct.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 111 About 50 meters ahead is {{ill|Aedo Hashihime Shrine|ja|饗土橋姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aedo Hashihime Shrine|qq=}}, and 70 meters ahead are the auxiliary shrines {{ill|Tsunaga Shrine|ja|津長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunaga Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Shinkawa Shrine|ja|新川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkawa Shrine|1=Nīkawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502105}}, and {{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Shrine|qq=}}, making a cluster of seven shrines.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 111, 116 The former {{ill|Hayasakibunko|ja|林崎文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasakibunko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神宮文庫|1=Jingū Bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589490}}, predecessor of the {{ill|Jingū Library|ja|神宮文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Library|qq=}}, is also located here.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 117 After ascending 12 stone steps, there is a {{ill|shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shinmei torii|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥居|1=torii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q234731}} and a single shrine building where all three shrines are co-located. An ancient camphor tree with a trunk diameter over 2 meters covers the shrine.Gakken Publishing (2013): pp. 54–55 The shrine, built in [[shinmei-zukuri]] style with plank roofing, faces south and is surrounded by a single [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] (sacred fence).Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 245 The shrine measures approximately 1.8 m wide, 1.2 m deep, and 2.4 m high. No {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|WD=Q135491444}} (offering box) is present. The shrine grounds cover 228 m² (approximately 69 tsubo).Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 244 According to the ''Enryaku Gishikichō,'' the ancient precinct covered about 24,793 m² but has been reduced due to encroachments from private houses, temples, and the Hayasakibunko. Parts of the grounds on the north and east sides are lined with [[:en:Stone wall|stone walls]]. == Ōmizu Shrine == Ōmizu Shrine is Ranked 16th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–10 The enshrined deity is Ōyamazumi-no-Mioya-no-Mikoto (大山祇御祖命), sometimes written as "大山'''罪乃'''御祖命" in old texts.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 243 This deity is considered a child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, worshipped as a mountain deity. Despite its name, meaning "great water shrine," the enshrined deity is a mountain god, which has puzzled some scholars. == Kawaai Shrine == Kawaai Shrine is Ranked 10th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 13–14 The enshrined deity is Hosokawa Mizu-no-Kami (細川水神), literally "narrow river water deity," a river god. == Kumabuchi Shrine == Kumabuchi Shrine is Ranked 11th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine. The enshrined deity is Takiootogi-no-Kami (多支大刀自神), a guardian deity of sacred springs, considered a child of Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命). == History == According to the ''Jingū Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines Register'' issued in 1880 (Meiji 13), the shrine was founded during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]. The ''Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō'' records that a {{ill|shukube|ja|祝部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shukube|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)|1=Hōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24869239}} was appointed to the shrine, with reconstruction handled by local officials. A record from 1482 confirms that the shrine still existed at that time. After falling into disrepair in the medieval period, the shrine was revived in 1664 under the leadership of {{ill|Kawabe no Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe no Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In 1871, Kawaai Shrine and Kumabuchi Shrine were moved to share the same grounds as Ōmizu Shrine. These two shrines were originally located upstream along the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]]. == Rituals == The rituals at Ōmizu Shrine follow the same schedule as those at {{ill|Tsunaga Shrine|ja|津長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunaga Shrine|qq=}}. Ceremonies are held during the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinen-sai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June & December), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October), and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] (November).Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 250 Other rituals, such as the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January 3), National Foundation Day Festival (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q72318}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|1=Kazahi-no-Misai|2=ja|3=風日祈祭|WD=Q135524493|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524493}} (May & August), are conducted remotely. == Flora == A 1977 survey of the shrine grounds identified 30 trees, including camphor, cedar, fig, chinquapin, and Japanese yew. == Access == ; To Uji Bridge * About 15 minutes by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus from [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] (JR) or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Get off at the "Naikū-mae" stop.Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Information]" (Accessed October 13, 2013) * Accessible from [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] via Mie Kotsu bus. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} or [[:en:Japan National Route 23|National Route 23]] from {{ill|Ise IC|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise IC|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}}. Parking is available nearby. ; From Uji Bridge * About 5 minutes (240 m) on foot from Uji Bridge rotary and Naikū-mae parking lot. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise Jingu|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == Notes == {{Footnotes help}} {{Reflist|2}} == References == * Ise Bunka-sha (ed.) ''O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Pilgrimage'' (Supplement to ''Isejin''), Ise Bunka-sha, December 23, 2008, 151 pages. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Office (ed.) ''History of Ujiyamada City, Volume 1'', Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929, 862 pages. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakken Publishing|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to Gakken|1=Gakken Plus Co., Ltd.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86741099}} ''Let's Go to Ise Jingu'', Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoru Sonoda|1=Minoru Sonoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622218}}, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82 pages. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * {{ill|Shikinaisha Research Association|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft研究会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha Research Association|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (ed.) ''Shikinaisha Survey Report, Volume 6 Tōkaidō 1'', Kōgakkan University Press, February 28, 1990, 690 pages. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0103.html Ōmizu Shrine (大水神社) - Co-located with Kawai Shrine and Kumafuchi Shrine] - Japan Ise Jingu Devotees Association Foundation {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|大水神社|version=95619690|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] }} [[ja:大水神社]] [[ja:Ōmizu Shrine]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Ōmizu Shrine (Q17223392)''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. revision.comment: Text replacement - "is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō." to "is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]."
revision.text:
[[ja:大水神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17223392]] Wikidata name: Ōmizu Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōmizu Shrine, Kawaai Shrine, Kumabuchi Shrine |image= [[File:Ōmizu-jinja 01.jpg|270px]] |location= [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], Uji Imazai-kechō, Hayasakiguchi 163-1, [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 27 |latitude_sec = 33.8 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 43 |longitude_sec = 17.5 |map= Japan Mie |enshrined_deity= Ōmizu Shrine: Ōyamazumi-no-Mioya-no-Mikoto
Kawaai Shrine: Hosokawa Mizu-no-Kami
Kumabuchi Shrine: Takiootogi-no-Kami |status= Ōmizu Shrine: {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine
Kawaai Shrine & Kumabuchi Shrine: Kōtai Jingū subordinate shrines |established= Traditionally attributed to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] |main_hall_style= [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |alternate_names= |sacred_sites= |annual_festival= |rituals= [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] }} '''Ōmizu Shrine''' (大水神社) is an auxiliary shrine of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|zh|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. This article also covers the subordinate shrines '''Kawaai Shrine''' (川相神社) and '''Kumabuchi Shrine''' (熊淵神社), which are co-located with Ōmizu Shrine. All three shrines are said to have been established by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]. Ōmizu Shrine enshrines a {{ill|Mountain God|ja|山の神|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mountain God|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Yama-no-Kami|WD=Q839254}}, while Kawaai Shrine and Kumabuchi Shrine enshrine [[:en:Suijin|water god]].Gakken Publishing (2013): pp. 54–55 == Overview == These three shrines are located at Uji Imazai-kechō, Hayasakiguchi 163-1, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], about 200 meters from the {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise City)|lt=Uji Bridge (Ise City)|lt=Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji Bridge (Ise)|qq=}} at the entrance to the Inner Shrine's sacred precinct.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 111 About 50 meters ahead is {{ill|Aedo Hashihime Shrine|ja|饗土橋姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aedo Hashihime Shrine|qq=}}, and 70 meters ahead are the auxiliary shrines {{ill|Tsunaga Shrine|ja|津長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunaga Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Shinkawa Shrine|ja|新川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkawa Shrine|1=Nīkawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502105}}, and {{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Shrine|qq=}}, making a cluster of seven shrines.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 111, 116 The former {{ill|Hayasakibunko|ja|林崎文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasakibunko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神宮文庫|1=Jingū Bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589490}}, predecessor of the {{ill|Jingū Library|ja|神宮文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Library|qq=}}, is also located here.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 117 After ascending 12 stone steps, there is a {{ill|shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shinmei torii|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥居|1=torii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q234731}} and a single shrine building where all three shrines are co-located. An ancient camphor tree with a trunk diameter over 2 meters covers the shrine.Gakken Publishing (2013): pp. 54–55 The shrine, built in [[shinmei-zukuri]] style with plank roofing, faces south and is surrounded by a single [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] (sacred fence).Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 245 The shrine measures approximately 1.8 m wide, 1.2 m deep, and 2.4 m high. No {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|WD=Q135491444}} (offering box) is present. The shrine grounds cover 228 m² (approximately 69 tsubo).Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 244 According to the ''Enryaku Gishikichō,'' the ancient precinct covered about 24,793 m² but has been reduced due to encroachments from private houses, temples, and the Hayasakibunko. Parts of the grounds on the north and east sides are lined with [[:en:Stone wall|stone walls]]. == Ōmizu Shrine == Ōmizu Shrine is Ranked 16th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–10 The enshrined deity is Ōyamazumi-no-Mioya-no-Mikoto (大山祇御祖命), sometimes written as "大山'''罪乃'''御祖命" in old texts.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 243 This deity is considered a child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, worshipped as a mountain deity. Despite its name, meaning "great water shrine," the enshrined deity is a mountain god, which has puzzled some scholars. == Kawaai Shrine == Kawaai Shrine is Ranked 10th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 13–14 The enshrined deity is Hosokawa Mizu-no-Kami (細川水神), literally "narrow river water deity," a river god. == Kumabuchi Shrine == Kumabuchi Shrine is Ranked 11th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine. The enshrined deity is Takiootogi-no-Kami (多支大刀自神), a guardian deity of sacred springs, considered a child of Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命). == History == According to the ''Jingū Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines Register'' issued in 1880 (Meiji 13), the shrine was founded during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]. The ''Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō'' records that a {{ill|shukube|ja|祝部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shukube|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)|1=Hōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24869239}} was appointed to the shrine, with reconstruction handled by local officials. A record from 1482 confirms that the shrine still existed at that time. After falling into disrepair in the medieval period, the shrine was revived in 1664 under the leadership of {{ill|Kawabe no Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe no Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In 1871, Kawaai Shrine and Kumabuchi Shrine were moved to share the same grounds as Ōmizu Shrine. These two shrines were originally located upstream along the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]]. == Rituals == The rituals at Ōmizu Shrine follow the same schedule as those at {{ill|Tsunaga Shrine|ja|津長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunaga Shrine|qq=}}. Ceremonies are held during the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinen-sai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June & December), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October), and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] (November).Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 250 Other rituals, such as the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January 3), National Foundation Day Festival (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q72318}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|1=Kazahi-no-Misai|2=ja|3=風日祈祭|WD=Q135524493|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524493}} (May & August), are conducted remotely. == Flora == A 1977 survey of the shrine grounds identified 30 trees, including camphor, cedar, fig, chinquapin, and Japanese yew. == Access == ; To Uji Bridge * About 15 minutes by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus from [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] (JR) or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Get off at the "Naikū-mae" stop.Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Information]" (Accessed October 13, 2013) * Accessible from [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] via Mie Kotsu bus. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} or [[:en:Japan National Route 23|National Route 23]] from {{ill|Ise IC|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise IC|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}}. Parking is available nearby. ; From Uji Bridge * About 5 minutes (240 m) on foot from Uji Bridge rotary and Naikū-mae parking lot. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise Jingu|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == Notes == {{Footnotes help}} {{Reflist|2}} == References == * Ise Bunka-sha (ed.) In 1871, Kawaai Shrine and Kumabuchi Shrine were moved to share the same grounds as Ōmizu Shrine. These two shrines were originally located upstream along the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]]. == Rituals == The rituals at Ōmizu Shrine follow the same schedule as those at {{ill|Tsunaga Shrine|ja|津長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunaga Shrine|qq=}}. Ceremonies are held during the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinen-sai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June & December), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October), and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] (November).Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 250 Other rituals, such as the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January 3), National Foundation Day Festival (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q72318}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|1=Kazahi-no-Misai|2=ja|3=風日祈祭|WD=Q135524493|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524493}} (May & August), are conducted remotely. == Flora == A 1977 survey of the shrine grounds identified 30 trees, including camphor, cedar, fig, chinquapin, and Japanese yew. == Access == ; To Uji Bridge * About 15 minutes by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus from [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] (JR) or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Get off at the "Naikū-mae" stop.Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Information]" (Accessed October 13, 2013) * Accessible from [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] via Mie Kotsu bus. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} or [[:en:Japan National Route 23|National Route 23]] from {{ill|Ise IC|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise IC|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}}. Parking is available nearby. ; From Uji Bridge * About 5 minutes (240 m) on foot from Uji Bridge rotary and Naikū-mae parking lot. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise Jingu|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == Notes == {{Footnotes help}} {{Reflist|2}} == References == * Ise Bunka-sha (ed.) ''O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Pilgrimage'' (Supplement to ''Isejin''), Ise Bunka-sha, December 23, 2008, 151 pages. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Office (ed.) ''History of Ujiyamada City, Volume 1'', Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929, 862 pages. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakken Publishing|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to Gakken|1=Gakken Plus Co., Ltd.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86741099}} ''Let's Go to Ise Jingu'', Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoru Sonoda|1=Minoru Sonoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622218}}, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82 pages. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * {{ill|Shikinaisha Research Association|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft研究会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha Research Association|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (ed.) ''Shikinaisha Survey Report, Volume 6 Tōkaidō 1'', Kōgakkan University Press, February 28, 1990, 690 pages. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0103.html Ōmizu Shrine (大水神社) - Co-located with Kawai Shrine and Kumafuchi Shrine] - Japan Ise Jingu Devotees Association Foundation {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|大水神社|version=95619690|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] }} [[ja:大水神社]] [[ja:Ōmizu Shrine]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Ōmizu Shrine (Q17223392)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! revision.text:
[[ja:大水神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17223392]] Wikidata name: Ōmizu Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōmizu Shrine, Kawaai Shrine, Kumabuchi Shrine |image= [[File:Ōmizu-jinja 01.jpg|270px]] |location= [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], Uji Imazai-kechō, Hayasakiguchi 163-1, [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 27 |latitude_sec = 33.8 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 43 |longitude_sec = 17.5 |map= Japan Mie |enshrined_deity= Ōmizu Shrine: Ōyamazumi-no-Mioya-no-Mikoto
Kawaai Shrine: Hosokawa Mizu-no-Kami
Kumabuchi Shrine: Takiootogi-no-Kami |status= Ōmizu Shrine: {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine
Kawaai Shrine & Kumabuchi Shrine: Kōtai Jingū subordinate shrines |established= Traditionally attributed to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] |main_hall_style= [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |alternate_names= |sacred_sites= |annual_festival= |rituals= [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] }} '''Ōmizu Shrine''' (大水神社) is an auxiliary shrine of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|zh|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. This article also covers the subordinate shrines '''Kawaai Shrine''' (川相神社) and '''Kumabuchi Shrine''' (熊淵神社), which are co-located with Ōmizu Shrine. All three shrines are said to have been established by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]. Ōmizu Shrine enshrines a {{ill|Mountain God|ja|山の神|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mountain God|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Yama-no-Kami|WD=Q839254}}, while Kawaai Shrine and Kumabuchi Shrine enshrine [[:en:Suijin|water god]].Gakken Publishing (2013): pp. 54–55 == Overview == These three shrines are located at Uji Imazai-kechō, Hayasakiguchi 163-1, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], about 200 meters from the {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise City)|lt=Uji Bridge (Ise City)|lt=Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji Bridge (Ise)|qq=}} at the entrance to the Inner Shrine's sacred precinct.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 111 About 50 meters ahead is {{ill|Aedo Hashihime Shrine|ja|饗土橋姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aedo Hashihime Shrine|qq=}}, and 70 meters ahead are the auxiliary shrines {{ill|Tsunaga Shrine|ja|津長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunaga Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Shinkawa Shrine|ja|新川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkawa Shrine|1=Nīkawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502105}}, and {{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Shrine|qq=}}, making a cluster of seven shrines.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 111, 116 The former {{ill|Hayasakibunko|ja|林崎文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasakibunko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神宮文庫|1=Jingū Bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589490}}, predecessor of the {{ill|Jingū Library|ja|神宮文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Library|qq=}}, is also located here.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 117 After ascending 12 stone steps, there is a {{ill|shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shinmei torii|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥居|1=torii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q234731}} and a single shrine building where all three shrines are co-located. An ancient camphor tree with a trunk diameter over 2 meters covers the shrine.Gakken Publishing (2013): pp. 54–55 The shrine, built in [[shinmei-zukuri]] style with plank roofing, faces south and is surrounded by a single [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] (sacred fence).Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 245 The shrine measures approximately 1.8 m wide, 1.2 m deep, and 2.4 m high. No {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|WD=Q135491444}} (offering box) is present. The shrine grounds cover 228 m² (approximately 69 tsubo).Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 244 According to the ''Enryaku Gishikichō,'' the ancient precinct covered about 24,793 m² but has been reduced due to encroachments from private houses, temples, and the Hayasakibunko. Parts of the grounds on the north and east sides are lined with [[:en:Stone wall|stone walls]]. == Ōmizu Shrine == Ōmizu Shrine is Ranked 16th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–10 The enshrined deity is Ōyamazumi-no-Mioya-no-Mikoto (大山祇御祖命), sometimes written as "大山'''罪乃'''御祖命" in old texts.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 243 This deity is considered a child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, worshipped as a mountain deity. Despite its name, meaning "great water shrine," the enshrined deity is a mountain god, which has puzzled some scholars. == Kawaai Shrine == Kawaai Shrine is Ranked 10th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 13–14 The enshrined deity is Hosokawa Mizu-no-Kami (細川水神), literally "narrow river water deity," a river god. == Kumabuchi Shrine == Kumabuchi Shrine is Ranked 11th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine. The enshrined deity is Takiootogi-no-Kami (多支大刀自神), a guardian deity of sacred springs, considered a child of Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命). == History == According to the ''Jingū Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines Register'' issued in 1880 (Meiji 13), the shrine was founded during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]. The ''Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō'' records that a {{ill|shukube|ja|祝部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shukube|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)|1=Hōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24869239}} was appointed to the shrine, with reconstruction handled by local officials. A record from 1482 confirms that the shrine still existed at that time. After falling into disrepair in the medieval period, the shrine was revived in 1664 under the leadership of {{ill|Kawabe no Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe no Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In 1871, Kawaai Shrine and Kumabuchi Shrine were moved to share the same grounds as Ōmizu Shrine. These two shrines were originally located upstream along the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]]. == Rituals == The rituals at Ōmizu Shrine follow the same schedule as those at {{ill|Tsunaga Shrine|ja|津長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunaga Shrine|qq=}}. Ceremonies are held during the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinen-sai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June & December), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October), and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] (November).Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 250 Other rituals, such as the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January 3), National Foundation Day Festival (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q72318}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|1=Kazahi-no-Misai|2=ja|3=風日祈祭|WD=Q135524493|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524493}} (May & August), are conducted remotely. == Flora == A 1977 survey of the shrine grounds identified 30 trees, including camphor, cedar, fig, chinquapin, and Japanese yew. == Access == ; To Uji Bridge * About 15 minutes by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus from [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] (JR) or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Get off at the "Naikū-mae" stop.Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Information]" (Accessed October 13, 2013) * Accessible from [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] via Mie Kotsu bus. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} or [[:en:Japan National Route 23|National Route 23]] from {{ill|Ise IC|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise IC|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}}. Parking is available nearby. ; From Uji Bridge * About 5 minutes (240 m) on foot from Uji Bridge rotary and Naikū-mae parking lot. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise Jingu|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == Notes == {{Footnotes help}} {{Reflist|2}} == References == * Ise Bunka-sha (ed.) ''O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Pilgrimage'' (Supplement to ''Isejin''), Ise Bunka-sha, December 23, 2008, 151 pages. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Office (ed.) ''History of Ujiyamada City, Volume 1'', Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929, 862 pages. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakken Publishing|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to Gakken|1=Gakken Plus Co., Ltd.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86741099}} ''Let's Go to Ise Jingu'', Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoru Sonoda|1=Minoru Sonoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622218}}, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82 pages. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * {{ill|Shikinaisha Research Association|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft研究会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha Research Association|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (ed.) ''Shikinaisha Survey Report, Volume 6 Tōkaidō 1'', Kōgakkan University Press, February 28, 1990, 690 pages. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0103.html Ōmizu Shrine (大水神社) - Co-located with Kawai Shrine and Kumafuchi Shrine] - Japan Ise Jingu Devotees Association Foundation {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|大水神社|version=95619690|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] }} [[ja:大水神社]] [[ja:Ōmizu Shrine]] {{wikidata link|Q17223392}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q17223392}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Ōmizu Shrine (Q17223392)''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. revision.text:
[[ja:大水神社]] [[ja:大水神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17223392]] Wikidata name: Ōmizu Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōmizu Shrine, Kawaai Shrine, Kumabuchi Shrine |image= [[File:Ōmizu-jinja 01.jpg|270px]] |location= [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], Uji Imazai-kechō, Hayasakiguchi 163-1, [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 27 |latitude_sec = 33.8 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 43 |longitude_sec = 17.5 |map= Japan Mie |enshrined_deity= Ōmizu Shrine: Ōyamazumi-no-Mioya-no-Mikoto
Kawaai Shrine: Hosokawa Mizu-no-Kami
Kumabuchi Shrine: Takiootogi-no-Kami |status= Ōmizu Shrine: {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine
Kawaai Shrine & Kumabuchi Shrine: Kōtai Jingū subordinate shrines |established= Traditionally attributed to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] |main_hall_style= [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |alternate_names= |sacred_sites= |annual_festival= |rituals= [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] }} '''Ōmizu Shrine''' (大水神社) is an auxiliary shrine of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|zh|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. This article also covers the subordinate shrines '''Kawaai Shrine''' (川相神社) and '''Kumabuchi Shrine''' (熊淵神社), which are co-located with Ōmizu Shrine. All three shrines are said to have been established by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]. Ōmizu Shrine enshrines a {{ill|Mountain God|ja|山の神|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mountain God|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Yama-no-Kami|WD=Q839254}}, while Kawaai Shrine and Kumabuchi Shrine enshrine [[:en:Suijin|water god]].Gakken Publishing (2013): pp. 54–55 == Overview == These three shrines are located at Uji Imazai-kechō, Hayasakiguchi 163-1, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], about 200 meters from the {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise City)|lt=Uji Bridge (Ise City)|lt=Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji Bridge (Ise)|qq=}} at the entrance to the Inner Shrine's sacred precinct.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 111 About 50 meters ahead is {{ill|Aedo Hashihime Shrine|ja|饗土橋姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aedo Hashihime Shrine|qq=}}, and 70 meters ahead are the auxiliary shrines {{ill|Tsunaga Shrine|ja|津長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunaga Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Shinkawa Shrine|ja|新川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkawa Shrine|1=Nīkawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502105}}, and {{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Shrine|qq=}}, making a cluster of seven shrines.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 111, 116 The former {{ill|Hayasakibunko|ja|林崎文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasakibunko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神宮文庫|1=Jingū Bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589490}}, predecessor of the {{ill|Jingū Library|ja|神宮文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Library|qq=}}, is also located here.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 117 After ascending 12 stone steps, there is a {{ill|shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shinmei torii|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥居|1=torii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q234731}} and a single shrine building where all three shrines are co-located. An ancient camphor tree with a trunk diameter over 2 meters covers the shrine.Gakken Publishing (2013): pp. 54–55 The shrine, built in [[shinmei-zukuri]] style with plank roofing, faces south and is surrounded by a single [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] (sacred fence).Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 245 The shrine measures approximately 1.8 m wide, 1.2 m deep, and 2.4 m high. No {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|WD=Q135491444}} (offering box) is present. The shrine grounds cover 228 m² (approximately 69 tsubo).Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 244 According to the ''Enryaku Gishikichō,'' the ancient precinct covered about 24,793 m² but has been reduced due to encroachments from private houses, temples, and the Hayasakibunko. Parts of the grounds on the north and east sides are lined with [[:en:Stone wall|stone walls]]. == Ōmizu Shrine == Ōmizu Shrine is Ranked 16th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–10 The enshrined deity is Ōyamazumi-no-Mioya-no-Mikoto (大山祇御祖命), sometimes written as "大山'''罪乃'''御祖命" in old texts.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 243 This deity is considered a child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, worshipped as a mountain deity. Despite its name, meaning "great water shrine," the enshrined deity is a mountain god, which has puzzled some scholars. == Kawaai Shrine == Kawaai Shrine is Ranked 10th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 13–14 The enshrined deity is Hosokawa Mizu-no-Kami (細川水神), literally "narrow river water deity," a river god. == Kumabuchi Shrine == Kumabuchi Shrine is Ranked 11th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine. The enshrined deity is Takiootogi-no-Kami (多支大刀自神), a guardian deity of sacred springs, considered a child of Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命). == History == According to the ''Jingū Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines Register'' issued in 1880 (Meiji 13), the shrine was founded during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]. The ''Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō'' records that a {{ill|shukube|ja|祝部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shukube|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)|1=Hōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24869239}} was appointed to the shrine, with reconstruction handled by local officials. A record from 1482 confirms that the shrine still existed at that time. After falling into disrepair in the medieval period, the shrine was revived in 1664 under the leadership of {{ill|Kawabe no Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe no Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In 1871, Kawaai Shrine and Kumabuchi Shrine were moved to share the same grounds as Ōmizu Shrine. These two shrines were originally located upstream along the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]]. == Rituals == The rituals at Ōmizu Shrine follow the same schedule as those at {{ill|Tsunaga Shrine|ja|津長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunaga Shrine|qq=}}. Ceremonies are held during the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinen-sai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June & December), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October), and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] (November).Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 250 Other rituals, such as the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January 3), National Foundation Day Festival (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q72318}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|1=Kazahi-no-Misai|2=ja|3=風日祈祭|WD=Q135524493|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524493}} (May & August), are conducted remotely. == Flora == A 1977 survey of the shrine grounds identified 30 trees, including camphor, cedar, fig, chinquapin, and Japanese yew. == Access == ; To Uji Bridge * About 15 minutes by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus from [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] (JR) or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Get off at the "Naikū-mae" stop.Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Information]" (Accessed October 13, 2013) * Accessible from [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] via Mie Kotsu bus. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} or [[:en:Japan National Route 23|National Route 23]] from {{ill|Ise IC|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise IC|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}}. Parking is available nearby. ; From Uji Bridge * About 5 minutes (240 m) on foot from Uji Bridge rotary and Naikū-mae parking lot. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise Jingu|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == Notes == {{Footnotes help}} {{Reflist|2}} == References == * Ise Bunka-sha (ed.) ''O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Pilgrimage'' (Supplement to ''Isejin''), Ise Bunka-sha, December 23, 2008, 151 pages. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Office (ed.) ''History of Ujiyamada City, Volume 1'', Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929, 862 pages. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakken Publishing|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to Gakken|1=Gakken Plus Co., Ltd.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86741099}} ''Let's Go to Ise Jingu'', Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoru Sonoda|1=Minoru Sonoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622218}}, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82 pages. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * {{ill|Shikinaisha Research Association|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft研究会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha Research Association|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (ed.) ''Shikinaisha Survey Report, Volume 6 Tōkaidō 1'', Kōgakkan University Press, February 28, 1990, 690 pages. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0103.html Ōmizu Shrine (大水神社) - Co-located with Kawai Shrine and Kumafuchi Shrine] - Japan Ise Jingu Devotees Association Foundation {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|大水神社|version=95619690|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] }} [[ja:大水神社]] [[ja:Ōmizu Shrine]] {{wikidata link|Q17223392}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q17223392}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Ōmizu Shrine (Q17223392)''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! In 1871, Kawaai Shrine and Kumabuchi Shrine were moved to share the same grounds as Ōmizu Shrine. These two shrines were originally located upstream along the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]]. == Rituals == The rituals at Ōmizu Shrine follow the same schedule as those at {{ill|Tsunaga Shrine|ja|津長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunaga Shrine|qq=}}. Ceremonies are held during the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinen-sai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June & December), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October), and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] (November).Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 250 Other rituals, such as the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January 3), National Foundation Day Festival (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q72318}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|1=Kazahi-no-Misai|2=ja|3=風日祈祭|WD=Q135524493|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524493}} (May & August), are conducted remotely. == Flora == A 1977 survey of the shrine grounds identified 30 trees, including camphor, cedar, fig, chinquapin, and Japanese yew. == Access == ; To Uji Bridge * About 15 minutes by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus from [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] (JR) or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Get off at the "Naikū-mae" stop.Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Information]" (Accessed October 13, 2013) * Accessible from [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] via Mie Kotsu bus. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} or [[:en:Japan National Route 23|National Route 23]] from {{ill|Ise IC|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise IC|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}}. Parking is available nearby. ; From Uji Bridge * About 5 minutes (240 m) on foot from Uji Bridge rotary and Naikū-mae parking lot. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise Jingu|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == Notes == {{Footnotes help}} {{Reflist|2}} == References == * Ise Bunka-sha (ed.) ''O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Pilgrimage'' (Supplement to ''Isejin''), Ise Bunka-sha, December 23, 2008, 151 pages. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Office (ed.) ''History of Ujiyamada City, Volume 1'', Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929, 862 pages. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakken Publishing|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to Gakken|1=Gakken Plus Co., Ltd.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86741099}} ''Let's Go to Ise Jingu'', Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoru Sonoda|1=Minoru Sonoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622218}}, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82 pages. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * {{ill|Shikinaisha Research Association|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft研究会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha Research Association|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (ed.) ''Shikinaisha Survey Report, Volume 6 Tōkaidō 1'', Kōgakkan University Press, February 28, 1990, 690 pages. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0103.html Ōmizu Shrine (大水神社) - Co-located with Kawai Shrine and Kumafuchi Shrine] - Japan Ise Jingu Devotees Association Foundation {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|大水神社|version=95619690|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] }} [[ja:大水神社]] [[ja:Ōmizu Shrine]] {{wikidata link|Q17223392}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q17223392}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Ōmizu Shrine (Q17223392)''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! revision.comment: Add page with conflicting Wikidata QIDs
revision.text:
[[ja:大水神社]] [[ja:大水神社]] [[ja:大水神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17223392]] Wikidata name: Ōmizu Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Ōmizu Shrine, Kawaai Shrine, Kumabuchi Shrine |image= [[File:Ōmizu-jinja 01.jpg|270px]] |location= [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], Uji Imazai-kechō, Hayasakiguchi 163-1, [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] |latitude_deg = 34 |latitude_min = 27 |latitude_sec = 33.8 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 43 |longitude_sec = 17.5 |map= Japan Mie |enshrined_deity= Ōmizu Shrine: Ōyamazumi-no-Mioya-no-Mikoto
Kawaai Shrine: Hosokawa Mizu-no-Kami
Kumabuchi Shrine: Takiootogi-no-Kami |status= Ōmizu Shrine: {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine
Kawaai Shrine & Kumabuchi Shrine: Kōtai Jingū subordinate shrines |established= Traditionally attributed to the reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] |main_hall_style= [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |alternate_names= |sacred_sites= |annual_festival= |rituals= [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] }} '''Ōmizu Shrine''' (大水神社) is an auxiliary shrine of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|zh|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. This article also covers the subordinate shrines '''Kawaai Shrine''' (川相神社) and '''Kumabuchi Shrine''' (熊淵神社), which are co-located with Ōmizu Shrine. All three shrines are said to have been established by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]. Ōmizu Shrine enshrines a {{ill|Mountain God|ja|山の神|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mountain God|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Yama-no-Kami|WD=Q839254}}, while Kawaai Shrine and Kumabuchi Shrine enshrine [[:en:Suijin|water god]].Gakken Publishing (2013): pp. 54–55 == Overview == These three shrines are located at Uji Imazai-kechō, Hayasakiguchi 163-1, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], about 200 meters from the {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise City)|lt=Uji Bridge (Ise City)|lt=Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji Bridge (Ise)|qq=}} at the entrance to the Inner Shrine's sacred precinct.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 111 About 50 meters ahead is {{ill|Aedo Hashihime Shrine|ja|饗土橋姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aedo Hashihime Shrine|qq=}}, and 70 meters ahead are the auxiliary shrines {{ill|Tsunaga Shrine|ja|津長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunaga Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Shinkawa Shrine|ja|新川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkawa Shrine|1=Nīkawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502105}}, and {{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Shrine|qq=}}, making a cluster of seven shrines.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): pp. 111, 116 The former {{ill|Hayasakibunko|ja|林崎文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasakibunko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神宮文庫|1=Jingū Bunko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11589490}}, predecessor of the {{ill|Jingū Library|ja|神宮文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Library|qq=}}, is also located here.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 117 After ascending 12 stone steps, there is a {{ill|shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shinmei torii|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥居|1=torii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q234731}} and a single shrine building where all three shrines are co-located. An ancient camphor tree with a trunk diameter over 2 meters covers the shrine.Gakken Publishing (2013): pp. 54–55 The shrine, built in [[shinmei-zukuri]] style with plank roofing, faces south and is surrounded by a single [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] (sacred fence).Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 245 The shrine measures approximately 1.8 m wide, 1.2 m deep, and 2.4 m high. No {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|WD=Q135491444}} (offering box) is present. The shrine grounds cover 228 m² (approximately 69 tsubo).Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 244 According to the ''Enryaku Gishikichō,'' the ancient precinct covered about 24,793 m² but has been reduced due to encroachments from private houses, temples, and the Hayasakibunko. Parts of the grounds on the north and east sides are lined with [[:en:Stone wall|stone walls]]. == Ōmizu Shrine == Ōmizu Shrine is Ranked 16th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 9–10 The enshrined deity is Ōyamazumi-no-Mioya-no-Mikoto (大山祇御祖命), sometimes written as "大山'''罪乃'''御祖命" in old texts.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 243 This deity is considered a child of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}, worshipped as a mountain deity. Despite its name, meaning "great water shrine," the enshrined deity is a mountain god, which has puzzled some scholars. == Kawaai Shrine == Kawaai Shrine is Ranked 10th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp. 13–14 The enshrined deity is Hosokawa Mizu-no-Kami (細川水神), literally "narrow river water deity," a river god. == Kumabuchi Shrine == Kumabuchi Shrine is Ranked 11th among the 16 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine. The enshrined deity is Takiootogi-no-Kami (多支大刀自神), a guardian deity of sacred springs, considered a child of Ōminakami-no-Mikoto (大水上命). == History == According to the ''Jingū Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines Register'' issued in 1880 (Meiji 13), the shrine was founded during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]. The ''Kōtai Jingū Gishikichō'' records that a {{ill|shukube|ja|祝部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shukube|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祝 (神職)|1=Hōri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24869239}} was appointed to the shrine, with reconstruction handled by local officials. A record from 1482 confirms that the shrine still existed at that time. After falling into disrepair in the medieval period, the shrine was revived in 1664 under the leadership of {{ill|Kawabe no Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe no Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In 1871, Kawaai Shrine and Kumabuchi Shrine were moved to share the same grounds as Ōmizu Shrine. These two shrines were originally located upstream along the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]]. == Rituals == The rituals at Ōmizu Shrine follow the same schedule as those at {{ill|Tsunaga Shrine|ja|津長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunaga Shrine|qq=}}. Ceremonies are held during the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinen-sai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June & December), [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October), and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]] (November).Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 250 Other rituals, such as the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}} (January 1), {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}} (January 3), National Foundation Day Festival (February 11), {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q72318}} (February 23), and {{ill|Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|1=Kazahi-no-Misai|2=ja|3=風日祈祭|WD=Q135524493|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze-no-hi-no-matsuri|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524493}} (May & August), are conducted remotely. == Flora == A 1977 survey of the shrine grounds identified 30 trees, including camphor, cedar, fig, chinquapin, and Japanese yew. == Access == ; To Uji Bridge * About 15 minutes by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus from [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] (JR) or {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Get off at the "Naikū-mae" stop.Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Grand Shrine (Inner Shrine) Information]" (Accessed October 13, 2013) * Accessible from [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] via Mie Kotsu bus. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} or [[:en:Japan National Route 23|National Route 23]] from {{ill|Ise IC|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise IC|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}}. Parking is available nearby. ; From Uji Bridge * About 5 minutes (240 m) on foot from Uji Bridge rotary and Naikū-mae parking lot. == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise Jingu|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise Jingu|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == Notes == {{Footnotes help}} {{Reflist|2}} == References == * Ise Bunka-sha (ed.) ''O-Ise-san 125 Shrine Pilgrimage'' (Supplement to ''Isejin''), Ise Bunka-sha, December 23, 2008, 151 pages. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * Ujiyamada City Office (ed.) ''History of Ujiyamada City, Volume 1'', Ujiyamada City Office, January 20, 1929, 862 pages. * {{ill|Gakken Publishing|ja|学研パブリッシング|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gakken Publishing|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to Gakken|1=Gakken Plus Co., Ltd.|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q86741099}} ''Let's Go to Ise Jingu'', Gakken Mook Shrine Travel Selection vol.1, supervised by {{ill|Minoru Sonoda|ja|薗田稔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minoru Sonoda|1=Minoru Sonoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11622218}}, Gakken Marketing, July 4, 2013, 82 pages. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * {{ill|Shikinaisha Research Association|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft研究会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha Research Association|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (ed.) ''Shikinaisha Survey Report, Volume 6 Tōkaidō 1'', Kōgakkan University Press, February 28, 1990, 690 pages. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0103.html Ōmizu Shrine (大水神社) - Co-located with Kawai Shrine and Kumafuchi Shrine] - Japan Ise Jingu Devotees Association Foundation {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|大水神社|version=95619690|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] }} [[ja:大水神社]] [[ja:Ōmizu Shrine]] {{wikidata link|Q17223392}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q17223392}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Ōmizu Shrine (Q17223392)''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō.
